mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer expenditure and residential investment, boosting aggregate demand and producing positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing activity, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of observations during tightening cycles when INDPRO declines, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Rank IC < 0.0).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 215.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns exceed zero in more than 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 215.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting wage growth and consumer demand, which drives positive corporate earnings and equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in over 50 percent of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly improves corporate profit margins and triggers upward earnings revisions, creating positive momentum that translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production amid high inflation signal stagflationary dynamics, prompting risk-off portfolio rebalancing and generating negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of stagflationary periods with consecutive INDPRO declines, or the threshold trigger fails to separate return distributions (t-test p > 0.05).
